text:
I woke up the other day feeling like I had slept through the lives of five people. I blinked into the morning light, feeling somehow forgotten. Rattled coffee in hand, I stumbled through breakfast. Something felt off, as if I'd lived a lifetime somewhere else.

After a long shower, I remembered. It was three weeks before our anniversary. I had gone out to breakfast that morning—a simple place by the river—and forgotten my phone. It wasn't until noon that I noticed. The whole date seemed like a dream; my girlfriend didn't seem surprised—I hadn't told her how I'd forgotten.

I called her. She was a mix of amused and amazed. I apologized profusely. Turns out, we've had so many vacations, we even have a handful of places we've just driven through without stopping. Our anniversary was merely another checkpoint.

We'd already booked another trip soon afterward, just like that.

That's where I was six months ago. Today, I'd
